title: Radiation treatment planning and execution
abstract: An apparatus for use in a treatment planning process or in a treatment process, includes: an input for obtaining a parameter representing a number of beam on-off transitions; and a treatment planner configured to optimize a treatment plan based on parameter representing the number of beam on-off transitions. An apparatus includes: an input configured to obtain a width of a gating window for a treatment plan; and a gating window adjustor configured to adjust the width of the gating window during a treatment session. An apparatus includes: a dose calculator configured to calculate doses for different treatment variations; an evaluator configured to evaluate treatment acceptance criteria against the calculated doses; and a delivery limit module configured to determine one or more limits for one or more delivery parameters based on an evaluation of the treatment acceptance criteria by the evaluator.
claims:
Claims What is claimed: 1. An apparatus for use in a treatment planning process or in a treatment process, comprising: an input for obtaining a parameter representing a number of beam on- off transitions; and a treatment planner configured to optimize a treatment plan based on parameter representing the number of beam on-off transitions. 2. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the beam on-off transitions comprise a transition from beam-on to beam-off, a transition from beam-off to beam-on, or both. 3. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the treatment planner is configured to optimize the treatment plan to reduce the number of beam on-off transitions. 4. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the treatment planner is configured to optimize the treatment plan during the treatment planning process. 5. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the treatment planner is configured to optimize the treatment plan during the treatment process. 6. The apparatus of claim 1 , further comprising a gating window adjustor configured to adjust a width of a gating window for the treatment plan. 7. The apparatus of claim 6, wherein the gating window adjustor is configured to adjust the width of the gating window during a treatment session. 8. The apparatus of claim 6, further comprising a motion data analyzer configured to analyze motion data associated with a target, wherein the gating window adjustor is configured to adjust the width of the gating window based on a result from the act of analyzing. 9. The apparatus of claim 6, further comprising a treatment progress monitor configured to determine a status parameter indicating a treatment progress, wherein the gating window adjustor is configured to adjust the width of the gating window based on the status parameter. 10. The apparatus of claim 6, further comprising a treatment duration estimator configured to determine an expected treatment duration based at least in part on a planned monitor unit and the width of the gating window. 11. The apparatus of claim 1 , further comprising a delivery limit module configured to determine a limit for a delivery parameter based on a treatment acceptance criterion. 12. The apparatus of claim 11 , wherein the delivery parameter comprises an isocenter position, a leaf position, a gantry angle, a collimator angle, a dose rate, a number of monitoring units, or any combination of the foregoing. 13. The apparatus of claim 11 , wherein the delivery limit module is configured to determine the limit automatically. 14. The apparatus of claim 11 , further comprising: a dose calculator configured to calculate doses for different treatment variations; and an evaluator configured to evaluate treatment acceptance criteria against the calculated doses, the treatment acceptance criteria including the treatment acceptance criterion; 

 wherein the delivery limit module is configured to determine the limit for the delivery parameter based on an evaluation of the treatment acceptance criteria by the evaluator. 15. A method for use in a treatment planning process or in a treatment process, comprising: obtaining, by an input, a parameter representing a number of beam on- off transitions; and optimizing, using a treatment planner, a treatment plan based on parameter representing the number of beam on-off transitions. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the beam on-off transitions comprise a transition from beam-on to beam-off, a transition from beam-off to beam-on, or both. 7. The method of claim 15, wherein the treatment plan is optimized to reduce the number of beam on-off transitions. 18. The method of claim 15, wherein the act of optimizing is performed during the treatment planning process. 19. The method of claim 15, wherein the act of optimizing is performed during the treatment process. 20. The method of claim 15, further comprising adjusting a width of a gating window for the treatment plan. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the act of adjusting the width of the gating window is performed during a treatment session. 22. The method of claim 20, further comprising analyzing motion data associated with a target, wherein the act of adjusting the width of the gating window is performed based on a result from the act of analyzing. 23. The method of claim 20, further comprising determining a status parameter indicating a treatment progress, wherein the act of adjusting the width of the gating window is performed based on the status parameter. 24. The method of claim 20, further comprising determining an expected treatment duration based at least in part on a planned monitor unit and the width of the gating window. 25. The method of claim 15, further comprising determining a limit for a delivery parameter based on a treatment acceptance criterion. 26. The method of claim 25, wherein the delivery parameter comprises an isocenter position, a leaf position, a gantry angle, a collimator angle, a dose rate, a number of monitoring units, or any combination of the foregoing. 27. The method of claim 25, wherein the act of determining the limit is performed automatically by a delivery limit module. 28. The method of claim 25, further comprising: calculating doses for different treatment variations; and evaluating treatment acceptance criteria against the calculated doses, the treatment acceptance criteria including the treatment acceptance criterion; wherein the limit for the delivery parameter is determined based on a result from the act of evaluating the treatment acceptance criteria. 29. An apparatus for use in a treatment planning process or in a treatment process, comprising: 

 an input configured to obtain a width of a gating window for a treatment plan; and a gating window adjustor configured to adjust the width of the gating window during a treatment session. 30. The apparatus of claim 29, further comprising a motion data analyzer configured to analyze motion data associated with a target, wherein the gating window adjustor is configured to adjust the width of the gating window based on an output from the motion data analyzer. 31. The apparatus of claim 30, wherein the output indicates a decrease in motion amplitude, and the gating window adjustor is configured to reduce the width of the gating window in correspondence with the decrease in the motion amplitude. 32. The apparatus of claim 30, wherein the output indicates an increase in motion amplitude, and the gating window adjustor is configured to increase the width of the gating window in correspondence with the increase in the motion amplitude. 33. The apparatus of claim 29, further comprising a treatment progress monitor configured to determine a status parameter indicating a treatment progress, wherein gating window adjustor is configured to adjust the width of the gating window based on the status parameter. 34. The apparatus of claim 33, wherein the status parameter indicates that the treatment progress is faster than a threshold, and the gating window adjustor is configured to reduce the width of the gating window in correspondence with the faster treatment progress. 35. The apparatus of claim 33, wherein the status parameter indicates that the treatment progress is slower than a threshold, and the gating window 

 adjustor is configured to increase the width of the gating window in correspondence with the slower treatment progress. 36. The apparatus of claim 29, further comprising a treatment duration estimator configured to determine an expected treatment duration based at least in part on a planned monitor unit and the width of the gating window. 37. A method for use in a treatment planning process or in a treatment process, comprising: obtaining, by an input, a width of a gating window for a treatment plan; and adjusting, using a gating window adjustor, the width of the gating window during a treatment session. 38. The method of claim 37, further comprising analyzing motion data associated with a target, wherein the act of adjusting the width of the gating window is performed based on a result from the act of analyzing. 39. The method of claim 38, wherein the result from the act of analyzing indicates a decrease in motion amplitude, and the width of the gating window is reduced in correspondence with the decrease in the motion amplitude. 40. The method of claim 38, wherein the result from the act of analyzing indicates an increase in motion amplitude, and the width of the gating window is increased in correspondence with the increase in the motion amplitude. 41. The method of claim 37, further comprising determining a status parameter indicating a treatment progress, wherein the act of adjusting the width of the gating window is performed based on the status parameter. 42. The method of claim 41 , wherein the status parameter indicates that the treatment progress is faster than a threshold, and the width of the gating window is reduced in correspondence with the faster treatment progress. 43. The method of claim 41 , wherein the status parameter indicates that the treatment progress is slower than a threshold, and the width of the gating window is increased in correspondence with the slower treatment progress. 44. The method of claim 37, further comprising determining an expected treatment duration based at least in part on a planned monitor unit and the width of the gating window. 45. An apparatus for use in a treatment planning process or in a treatment process, comprising: a dose calculator configured to calculate doses for different treatment variations; an evaluator configured to evaluate treatment acceptance criteria against the calculated doses; and a delivery limit module configured to determine one or more limits for one or more delivery parameters based on an evaluation of the treatment acceptance criteria by the evaluator. 46. The apparatus of claim 45, wherein the delivery parameter comprises an isocenter position, a leaf position, a gantry angle, a collimator angle, a dose rate, a number of monitoring units, or any combination of the foregoing. 47. The apparatus of claim 45, wherein the delivery limit module is configured to determine the one or more limits automatically. 48. The apparatus of claim 45, wherein the treatment acceptance criterion comprises a DVH criterion. 49. A method for use in a treatment planning process or in a treatment process, comprising: calculating, by a dose calculator, doses for different treatment variations; evaluating, by an evaluator, treatment acceptance criteria against the calculated doses; and determining, by a delivery limit module, one or more limits for one or more delivery parameters based on a result from the act of evaluating the treatment acceptance criteria. 50. The method of claim 49, wherein the delivery parameter comprises an isocenter position, a leaf position, a gantry angle, a collimator angle, a dose rate, a number of monitoring units, or any combination of the foregoing. 51. The method of claim 49, wherein the act of determining the one or more limits is performed automatically by the delivery limit module. 52. The method of claim 49, wherein the treatment acceptance criterion comprises a DVH criterion.
